authority: local Bundjil decision and routing writes, read-only GitHub repository-settings readback, and local capability inspection only; no provider, workflow, secret, deployment, or GitHub mutation
acceptedOutcome: Four reversible local defaults and one claim-specific Eve live-state deferral govern Bundjil's runbook ownership, Claude review containment, boundary exceptions, provider fallbacks, and external-state nonclaims before HGI-302 through HGI-306 implement them.

decisions.outcome: No Eve Production claim is accepted; HGI-303 may obtain the readback and HGI-305 may retain inconclusive proof without a task-wide block.
Operational procedures live in apps/agent/runbooks and apps/codex-proxy/runbooks; central docs only route and explain durable authority rationale.

The current automatic review is an ungoverned mutation-capable experiment; HGI-304 must prove and tighten comment authority and convergence or retire it, while HGI-306 must prove cost and false-positive admission.
Retain occurrence-based exceptions unless a semantic/generated replacement proves exact matching, duplicate rejection, occurrence isolation, and stale detection.
Use target-owned read-only fallbacks with identity, target, observedAt, sanitized shape, limitation, stop, escalation, and no mutation path; unavailable means inconclusive.

verification.check: direct decision-record schema and receipt validation
verification.result: All five records pass the canonical Draft 2020 decision schema, use instance-relative receipt paths, match the content-addressed receipt, and have exact affectedTasks/taskBlocking equality.

All five source hashes reproduce from base 11708ff; GitHub readback reproduced Actions enabled, allowed_actions=all, and sha_pinning_required=false, with no inference about tokens, comments, effectiveness, or Eve state.
Twenty-four focused tests prove exact admission, occurrence isolation, duplicate rejection, and stale-entry detection; boundary and skill audits pass.

The evaluator exposed one-way task mapping, a self-deadlocking deferral, wrong runbook path ownership, and understated Claude mutation risk. Corrected central dependencies/scopes and local records then received PASS.
Ultracite format/lint, boundary tests, boundary audit, skill audit, and git diff --check pass.

Effect setup, boundary and skill audits, 29 tooling tests, Ultracite, lint fixtures, Knip, five workspace typechecks, and 250 package/app tests passed with a schema-valid synthetic Executor test configuration and no provider call.

limitations: GitHub Actions settings do not prove workflow execution, token issuance, comment authority, review quality, cost, or convergence.
Vercel, Executor, Sendblue, Upstash, and secret-binding readbacks remain unavailable or intentionally unattempted pending target-owned authority.
The Eve live-state deferral prohibits claims but does not block HGI-303 runbook/readback work or HGI-305 inconclusive proof design.
nonClaims: No provider, workflow, GitHub setting, secret, deployment, webhook, message, credential, production, release, or publication state was mutated.
No accepted local default grants external authority or establishes provider actuality.
rollback: Revert this bounded HGI-308 candidate slice and the corresponding central task pointers together; preserve HGI-300, HGI-301, and their immutable receipts.
nextTrigger: Use the final Git/readback receipt to close HGI-308, then implement HGI-302 and HGI-310 before the app-owned HGI-303 runbooks.
